pages.full_text:
Tues October 28
Freezing cold night. Milk frozen in fridge. After taking down the 2 tents, we left at 9.45. Thomas has washed + dried breakfast each day in return for sleeping in his own tent.
     We reached Madame Tussauds at 10.30. to meet Rob, Elizabeth + Cattarine Hungley in the queue. But the queue was 1¼ hours long so we left them to walk to Regents Park instead.

[glued-in animal cut-outs: snail, red-and-black frog, elephant]

We saw horrendous snakes, amusing chimpanzees + beautiful giraffes. John + Charles got lost after going on the way back from the giraffe bouncy castle, so we were late meeting Rob + the girls in Regents Park. (Thomas had been shopping + bought a fleece.) We searched for somewhere to eat, Topkapi, which was prepar[d]
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. 'Cattarine Hungley' may be 'Catherine Hungley' or similar — transcribed as written. 'Topkapi' is a restaurant name (Turkish restaurant). The word at the bottom is cut off — reads 'prepar[d]' likely 'prepared' continuing onto the right page. Year inferred as 1997: October 28 fell on a Tuesday in 1997, and the right page transitions to '1998' for the next entry.

pages.full_text:
to feed 10 of us shish kebab in 30 minutes. Afterwards we took 2 taxis

[glued-in giraffe cut-out]

through heavy traffic to St. Martin's Theatre to see "the Mousetrap."
     We trained + bussed back to the van at Picketts Lock, + drove home, (arriving about 10?) buying sandwiches on the motorway.

__1998__

April 4th
     Noon we left home for Northamptonshi[re] Simon + Eve + Tonic, Peter (nearly 15) John (10) Charles (nearly 9). William is with the de Fusses in Lyons. Thomas is already on an A level revision course for music at Oundle.
     We turned off the A14 + had lunch at the village hall at Naseby - good, savings, gr[eat] for Tonic.
pages.notes: Page number 59 visible in top-right corner. The year '1998' appears as a heading/underlined separator between two entries. The word at top continues from the left page ('to feed 10 of us...'). 'de Fusses' may be 'de Fuscos' or similar — transcribed as best reading. 'Lyons' is likely Lyon, France. Text at bottom right is partly cut off — 'gr[eat]' is a reasonable guess. The ink changes to blue for the 1998 section. 'Tonic' appears to be a pet (dog). 'St. Martin's Theatre' and 'the Mousetrap' refer to Agatha Christie's The Mousetrap in London's West End.
